free_text: Estimation of double differential angle-dependent
neutron production cross sections from tritons on
197Au at energies from 5.97 to 19.14 MeV
Walden University, Minneapolis
Rev.9 (May, 2005)
Description of DROSG-2000v2.21
LANL Ion Beam Facility (IBF)
Gas target (0.49 mg/cm2) in a cell (3.0 cm length)
with a molybdenum entrance window (5.3 mg/cm2) and a
gold beam stop (0.076 cm thick)
The effective gold target thickness is the depth in
gold at which the triton beam has lost the same
amount of energy as in the gas of the gas-filled cell.
2" x 2" liquid  scintillator (NE213).
Efficiency at two pulse-height biases (minimum between
25 keV and 60 keV peak in 241Am gamma spectrum,
Compton edge of 137Cs) was determined by
1) measuring 2H(t,n)4He and 1H(t,n)3He neutron yields
at about 30 neutron energies between 2.2 and 36.9 MeV;
2) comparing them with DROSG-2000v2.21
Flight path of 3.61 m, the total time resolution
of 2.1 nsec (FWHM of prompt gamma-ray peak)
Corrections for the neutron interaction in gold, in
the target structure and in the air of the flight
path were obtained by means of a Monte Carlo
technique.
Corrected and approved by Manfred Drosg
Data received from Manfred Drosg
On
Corrected by Manfred Drosg
On. REFERENCE: NSE vol#, page#, year added.
The neutron yield for Au was obtained by the ratio
between the spectrum of the empty cell and that of
a cell filled with hydrogen multiplied by the factor
[m(Au)*dE(Au)]/[m(H)*dE(H)],
where m and dE are the molar mass and energy loss,
respectively.
Counting statistics
Scale uncertainty
Data received from Manfred Drosg
This small statistical uncertainty just means
that 10E+6 counts have been recorded in this spectrum
